name: Rock paintings of Abrics - Circular tour of the Godall sierra
name_original: Pinturas rupestres de Abrics - Vuelta circular por la sierra de Godall
region_intro: Hiking around Godall offers diverse natural landscapes, primarily characterized by the Serra de Godall mountain range and the expansive Parc Natural dels Ports. The region features varied terrain, including hills and countryside views, with elevations suitable for different hiking abilities. The area is known for its natural beauty, providing opportunities to explore local flora and fauna. Best hiking trails around Godall The most popular hiking route is Rock paintings of Abrics - Circular tour of the Godall sierra, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 33 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Godall sierra, featuring rock paintings and natural scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Torreta del Montsià – Foradada Arch loop from los Campets, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.3 km) path. This trail offers views of the Serra de Montsià and the distinctive Foradada Arch. Local hikers also love the Torreta de Montsià (763 m) – Lo Fornet Well loop from los Campets, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail leading through the Serra de Montsià, often completed in about 3 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around Godall is defined by the diverse landscapes of the Parc Natural dels Ports, the Serra de Godall, and scenic countryside views. The network of routes offers options for easy walks and more challenging moderate trails. The routes in Godall are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1700 hikers have used komoot to explore Godall's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Godall?
A: There are over 100 hiking trails around Godall, offering a wide range of options for different abilities and preferences. You'll find everything from easy strolls to more challenging routes through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Godall?
A: Hiking in Godall primarily features the varied terrain of the Serra de Godall mountain range and the expansive Parc Natural dels Ports. You can expect a mix of hills, countryside views, and natural landscapes, with elevations suitable for various hiking abilities. The area is known for its natural beauty, offering opportunities to explore local flora and fauna.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hikes in Godall?
A: Godall offers a good mix of difficulty levels. You'll find approximately 27 easy routes, 71 moderate routes, and a few more challenging trails, with 4 difficult routes available. This variety ensures there's a suitable hike for almost every experience level.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Godall?
A: The trails in Godall are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 230 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the natural beauty of the Parc Natural dels Ports, and the scenic countryside views that enhance the hiking experience.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly hikes in Godall?
A: Yes, Godall offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17511919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Sant Lluc – Ulldecona Town Center loop from Ulldecona - Alcanar - La Sénia&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.3 km (3.9 miles) trail that takes about 1 hour 37 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Godall?
A: Yes, many routes in Godall are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952586882/rock-paintings-of-abrics-circular-tour-of-the-godall-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Abrics - Circular tour of the Godall sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 15.8 km (9.8 miles) loop exploring the Godall sierra and its rock paintings.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails in Godall?
A: Godall's hiking trails offer access to several notable natural features and landmarks. You can explore the diverse ecosystems of the Parc Natural dels Ports and the Serra de Godall. Highlights include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/678413" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Foradada Arch — View of the Ebro Delta&lt;/a&gt;, the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4583989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ullals de Baltasar&lt;/a&gt;, and various caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/327581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova d'en Marc&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4324720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remei Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning panoramic views.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer great viewpoints in Godall?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Godall provide rewarding viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23759647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreta del Montsià – Foradada Arch loop from los Campets&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Serra de Montsià and the distinctive Foradada Arch. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4324720" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remei Hermitage&lt;/a&gt; is a destination known for its stunning panoramic views of the surrounding countryside.

Q: Can I find trails suitable for birdwatching in Godall?
A: Yes, the Serra de Godall mountain range is ideal for birdwatching, offering beautiful natural surroundings. The diverse ecosystems within the Parc Natural dels Ports also provide excellent opportunities to observe local flora and fauna, including various bird species.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Godall?
A: Based on the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the best times for hiking in Godall. The weather is typically milder and more pleasant, making it ideal for exploring the natural parks and mountain ranges without the intense heat of summer or the cooler temperatures of winter.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to historical or cultural sites?
A: Yes, some trails in Godall offer a blend of nature and culture. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952586882/rock-paintings-of-abrics-circular-tour-of-the-godall-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock paintings of Abrics - Circular tour of the Godall sierra&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, takes you past ancient rock paintings, offering a glimpse into the region's past while enjoying the natural scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging routes for experienced hikers in Godall?
A: While Godall has a majority of moderate routes, there are 4 difficult trails available for experienced hikers seeking a greater challenge. These routes typically feature more significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a more strenuous outdoor experience.
tips: The Abrics d'Ermites, or Coves de l'Ermita, are a set of ten rocky shelters in the municipality of Ulldecona (Montsià) with representations of cave paintings protected as World Heritage
The number of paintings preserved is exceptionally beautiful.
Serra de Godall is the last Catalan Coastal mountain range, above the town of Ulldecona and just behind Montsià
